top_sources.top_context: enuinely struggle to eat + need appetite help before meals - Most people have opposite problem ### Better alternative - **Ipamorelin + CJC-1295 no DAC** - All ghrelin/pituitary benefits without prolactin spike, cortisol bump, or hunger - Stronger, more sustained, more selective GH release ## #3 — HGH Fragment 176-191 ### Why it's overrated - Unmodified C-terminal fragment of HGH (basically AOD without tyrosine modification) - **Worse than AOD-9604** — AOD at least had clinical trials - HGH Frag = almost no human clinical data, marketed as "pure fat-burning fragment" - Still listed on many research site

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider whether HGH Fragment 176-191 belongs in the protocol at all: Hunter is the only one of the three experts with on-record content for this peptide, and his operative frame is that it is overrated and outclassed by alternatives at the same price point. Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:15.600): "HGH frag is 176 through 191. This is the unmodified C-terminal fragment of human growth hormone. Essentially, AOD without the tyrosine modification" — and Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:36.200): "AOD at least had clinical trials, HGH does not and there's almost no human clinical data." Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14): "HGH builds muscle. HGH frag only aids fat loss" — so we'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh whether the patient's goal is fat-loss-only and, if so, whether Hunter's swap-out — "HGH frag, same thing, tessa or a GLP" (Hunter ODWpYYPTB_8 00:16:35.440) — better serves them.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Almost no human clinical data: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:36.200): "AOD at least had clinical trials, HGH does not and there's almost no human clinical data."
- Worse than AOD-9604 in Hunter's frame: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:25.840): "I would even say it's worse than AOD"; Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:44.240): "things that it's AOD, but worse." Hunter email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d (2026-04-07) "Why it's overrated": "Worse than AOD-9604 — AOD at least had clinical trials" and "HGH Frag = almost no human clinical data, marketed as 'pure fat-burning fragment'."
- Fat-loss-only — does NOT build muscle: Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14): "Best peptide for muscle growth: HGH vs. HGH fragment 176-191? HGH builds muscle. HGH frag only aids fat loss."
- Hunter's explicit personal stance: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:47.120): "if you were going to use one of these, I would not use HGH frag."
- Marketing-driven adoption: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:22.480): "positioned as the pure fat fat burning HGH fragment"; Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:10:03.000): "they Google it, and they're like, oh, fat burning, and then they get it." Hunter email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d (2026-04-07): "Still listed on many research sites — people see 'fat burning fragment' and pull trigger."
- Not on Hunter's cheat sheet: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:10:08.760): "this would be one that is not on the peptide cheat sheet uh and not one that I talk about."
- No baseline_protocol exists for HGH Fragment 176-191 in Hunter's 2023 cheat sheet — the entire dose/timing/duration scaffold present for peptides like BPC-157 is absent here.
- Single-expert source: only Hunter has on-record content (21 mentions across podcast + emails). Taylor and Trevor produced no captured material on this peptide, so there is no triangulation available.
- Educational framing only: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:16:44.079): "this is educational and uh entertainment purposes only content. So, please take all this with a grain of salt."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: No tri-expert consensus available — Hunter is the only expert with captured content on HGH Fragment 176-191. Within Hunter's own corpus, the podcast and the 2026-04-07 newsletter agree: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:25.840): "I would even say it's worse than AOD" and Hunter email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d (2026-04-07): "Worse than AOD-9604 — AOD at least had clinical trials." Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:09:38.480): "there's almost no human clinical data" and Hunter email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d (2026-04-07): "HGH Frag = almost no human clinical data." Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:16:33.680): "HGH frag, same thing, tessa or a GLP" and Hunter email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d (2026-04-07) "Better alternative": "Tesamorelin for GH-side fat burning" and "Pair with GLP-1 agonist for meaningful sustained fat loss."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: No cross-expert disagreement to surface — Taylor Williams and Dr Trevor Bachmeyer have no captured content on HGH Fragment 176-191. Hunter does not contradict himself across the two same-day 2026-04-07 sources (podcast ODWpYYPTB_8 + newsletter threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d) or against the older 2025-02-14 newsletter threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df: "HGH builds muscle. HGH frag only aids fat loss." The only nuance is intensity — newer 2026-04-07 content adds the explicit "worse than AOD" framing that older 2025-02-14 content does not articulate, but neither contradicts the other. Newer overrides older: the operative recommendation is Hunter's 2026-04-07 swap-out — Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:16:33.680): "HGH frag, same thing, tessa or a GLP."

chronic.dose: No verbatim dose recommendation — Hunter does not give a number. Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14): "HGH frag only aids fat loss" (effect framing, not dose).
chronic.route: Not specified in Hunter's HGH-frag content. (For the comparator AOD-9604, Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14) says "Switch to IM (intramuscular) administration" when subQ causes lumps — but that quote is about AOD, not HGH frag, so we surface it only as adjacent context, not as an HGH-frag route claim.)
